failures.title: feat(control-ui): add cron calendar timeline view
acpx: add terminal-truth artifacts and strict terminal states
fix(feishu): emit sent hooks for normal replies
[Bug]: acpx flow run ACP node outputs return empty strings instead of agent replies
cron: allow retries for local model preflight
Slack channel sends fail: `reconcileUnknownSend` required but no channel adapter implements it
fix(subagents): notify user on announce give-up instead of silently dropping result
docs(queue): clarify steer behavior with partial streaming and tool boundaries
feat(status): add API call count to session status and usage footer
test: add regression tests for <final> tag stripping in UI message extraction
fix(cron): prevent premature session cleanup when subagents are running

topic_error_patterns.action: `reliability` mixed: expected in 10 rows, predicted in 11, TP=9, FP=2, FN=1, precision=0.818, recall=0.900. Both precision and recall need boundary work. Mixed `reliability` errors. a generic bug tag, CI-only or test-environment failures (`tests_ci`), or a failure that merely motivates a change whose deliverable belongs entirely to another surface. MUST include when central: timeout, crash, leak, stuck state, retry, data loss, lifecycle cleanup, recovery, overload, or operational failure mode.

`coding_agents` under_predicted: expected in 3 rows, predicted in 0, TP=0, FP=0, FN=3, precision=0.000, recall=0.000. Recall is the bottleneck. MUST include when central: integrations with external coding agents in general, or with a specific coding agent such as Codex, Claude Code, Gemini CLI, or Pi. Boundary: ACP is an integration protocol and is distinct from coding-agent integrations; route ACP protocol/session/delivery work to `acp`/`acpx`. OpenClaw's internal agent runtime is a core product surface; route startup, loop, backend, model-call orchestration, and adapter machinery to `agent_runtime`.

`notifications` mixed: expected in 5 rows, predicted in 3, TP=3, FP=0, FN=2, precision=1.000, recall=0.600. Both precision and recall need boundary work. Mixed `notifications` errors. chat-platform-specific behavior alone (`chat_integrations`) or reliability-only recovery. Co-label: when a chat-surface change implements or alters a delivery payload/path or delivery gates, add notifications alongside the chat label. MUST include when central: generic outbound notifications, completion delivery, message delivery gates, announcements, or notify behavior is central. Co-label: when a chat-surface change implements or alters a delivery payload/path or delivery gates, add notifications alongside the chat label.

`ui_tui` mixed: expected in 7 rows, predicted in 6, TP=6, FP=0, FN=1, precision=1.000, recall=0.857. Both precision and recall need boundary work. Mixed `ui_tui` errors. command internals, telemetry fields, or API behavior not shown to users. MUST include when central: UI/TUI display, status, footer, mobile UI, or visual interaction is central.

`cron_automation` mixed: expected in 3 rows, predicted in 2, TP=2, FP=0, FN=1, precision=1.000, recall=0.667. Both precision and recall need boundary work. Mixed `cron_automation` errors. merely because an agent/runtime heartbeat is mentioned. MUST include when central: cron jobs, heartbeat runs, scheduled automation, or force-run behavior is central.

`telemetry_usage` mixed: expected in 3 rows, predicted in 2, TP=2, FP=0, FN=1, precision=1.000, recall=0.667. Both precision and recall need boundary work. Mixed `telemetry_usage` errors. generic reliability or UI text without measurement/status data. MUST include when central: token counts, usage counts, costs, metrics, diagnostics, traces, or status reporting are central.

`gateway` mixed: expected in 1 rows, predicted in 2, TP=1, FP=1, FN=0, precision=0.500, recall=1.000. Both precision and recall need boundary work. Mixed `gateway` errors. ordinary provider proxy, HTTP compatibility, or app-runtime bugs unless the gateway is the owning boundary. MUST include when central: gateway routing, gateway state, gateway startup, gateway protocol, or gateway-owned execution is central.

`self_hosted_inference` mixed: expected in 1 rows, predicted in 0, TP=0, FP=0, FN=1, precision=0.000, recall=0.000. Both precision and recall need boundary work. Mixed `self_hosted_inference` errors. generic hosted inference API usage (`inference_api`), or model-artifact/hardware behavior (`local_models`). Boundary: "self-hosted" includes on-device engines; the boundary with `local_models` is engine integration vs model/hardware behavior. MUST include when central: integration with inference engines such as vLLM, llama.cpp, Ollama, LM Studio, TGI, or LocalAI — on device or self-hosted elsewhere — including engine setup, lifecycle, compatibility, engine crashes/timeouts, and self-hosted embeddings/speech/memory backends. Boundary: "self-hosted" includes on-device engines; the boundary with `local_models` is engine integration vs model/hardware behavior.

confusions.action: Clarify `coding_agents` vs `gateway`. For missed `coding_agents`: MUST include when central: integrations with external coding agents in general, or with a specific coding agent such as Codex, Claude Code, Gemini CLI, or Pi. Boundary: ACP is an integration protocol and is distinct from coding-agent integrations; route ACP protocol/session/delivery work to `acp`/`acpx`. OpenClaw's internal agent runtime is a core product surface; route startup, loop, backend, model-call orchestration, and adapter machinery to `agent_runtime`. For extra `gateway`: ordinary provider proxy, HTTP compatibility, or app-runtime bugs unless the gateway is the owning boundary.

actionable_feedback: Pure-F1 objective: optimize exact topic membership, not recall-biased F-beta. Current precision=0.944, recall=0.870, F1=0.905.
Cardinality diagnosis: under_labeling; avg predicted 2.22 vs expected 2.41. Add central co-labels when multiple maintainer interests are explicit; avoid single-label collapse.
Policy length penalty: policy is 1988 chars over the 4000 char budget; GEPA score was reduced by 0.0099.
Topic cardinality is close: avg predicted topics 2.22 vs expected 2.41. Focus on boundary-specific errors.

vanilla_f1_asi.reflection_hint: Make concise reusable routing rules. Do not copy the fixed allowed-topic taxonomy, do not add row-specific examples, and do not optimize for recall at the expense of F1.
